qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated upload endpoint allows arbitrary file write outside the intended resource directory
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding targets the unauthenticated upload path in `POST /api/upload`; the advisory is the heartbeat refresh JWT-minting auth bypass in `server/api/index.js` and `server/api/jwt-helper.js`.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Low-privilege users can mint admin-identity tokens through the heartbeat refresh path
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: It identifies the same vulnerable `POST /api/heartbeat` refresh path and `jwt-helper.js` trust of attacker-controlled `x-auth-user` that the advisory patch removes; the vulnerable code also falls back to a guest token when no JWT is supplied, making the advisory's unauthenticated variant reachable on the same root cause.

selected_finding.title: Low-privilege users can mint admin-identity tokens through the heartbeat refresh path

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Finding `002_low_privilege_admin_token_forgery` matches the advisory's heartbeat token-refresh auth bypass, and the copied runtime harness demonstrated unauthenticated minting of an `admin` JWT that was then accepted as admin on a protected follow-up authorization path.
evidence: Advisory fix commit `fe82348d160904d0013b9a3e267d50158f5c7afb` changes the same vulnerable files named by the finding, `server/api/index.js` and `server/api/jwt-helper.js`, by requiring an authenticated request for heartbeat refresh and by stopping token minting from `x-auth-user`.

In vulnerable v1.2.9, `verifyToken()` substitutes `getGuestToken()` when `x-access-token` is absent, and `/api/heartbeat` refreshes any request with `req.body.params` by calling `getNewToken(req.headers)`.

`/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-DryRun/advisory_cfdd2caf/.judge/poc_runs/002_low_privilege_admin_token_forgery/heartbeat_exploit_result.json` shows an unauthenticated request with `x-auth-user={"user":"admin","groups":"guest"}` received `message: "tokenRefresh"` and a minted JWT whose payload contains `id: "admin"`.

The same runtime artifact shows the forged JWT was accepted on a follow-up protected request, resolved `userId` to `admin`, and produced admin permission `-1`, so `haveAdminPermission()` returned `true`.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied harness used the vulnerable heartbeat refresh logic to return `message: "tokenRefresh"` for an unauthenticated request, minted a JWT with payload `id=admin`, and then showed a follow-up protected request resolving admin permission `-1` from the forged token.
